content.cn_text: 对于我的网站设计项目，我决定采用Supersave超市，因为我在超市有一份晚上的工作，所以我已经对他们的组织目标和运作有了一些了解。
content.en_text: For my website design project, I decided lo approach Supersave supermarkets, because I have an evening job at the supermarket, so I already have a slight insight into their organisational goals and workings.

content.cn_text: 我的项目的实地调研分为两个阶段。
content.en_text: The field research for my project was in two stages.

content.cn_text: 首先，我采访了Dunne先生，他是Supersave客户服务部门的负责人。
content.en_text: First, I had an interview with Mr Dunne, who is in charge of Supersave's customer care department.

content.cn_text: 为了确定超市的需求，我和他讨论了这个项目。
content.en_text: I discussed the project with him in order to identify the supermarket's requirements.

content.cn_text: 邓恩先生说，当顾客遇到产品、工作人员或与超市有关的任何问题时，他们通常不愿意当面投诉。
content.en_text: Mr Dunne said customers are often unwilling to make a face-to-face complaint when they've experienced difficulties with a product, or a member of staff, or anything related to the supermarket.

content.cn_text: 所以他说，建立一个网站，让公众与该组织取得联系，并以一种私人的方式引起他们的注意，这可能非常有用，我们同意我来做这件事。
content.en_text: So he said a website which allowed members of the public to get in touch with the organisation and bring the problem to their attention in a private manner might be very useful, and we agreed that I'd work on this.

content.cn_text: 在我研究的第二阶段，我设计了一份调查问卷
content.en_text: For the second stage of my research, I devised a questionnaire to put to

content.cn_text: 一下客户。
content.en_text: Supersave customers.

content.cn_text: 我需要了解客户遇到问题的经历，以及他们对直接和间接投诉的态度。
content.en_text: I needed to find out about the customers' experiences of problems,together with their attitudes towards making complaints, both directly and indirectly.

content.cn_text: 我用了一些封闭式问题，比如“你在超级超市遇到过问题吗?”
content.en_text: I used a mixture of closed questions such as 'Have you ever experienced a problem at any Supersave store?'

content.cn_text: 和开放式问题，如“你觉得客户投诉网站有什么帮助?””
content.en_text: and open questions such as 'What would you find helpful about a customer complaint website?'

content.cn_text: 我决定进行访谈，而不是依靠分发问卷，因为我觉得这可能会导致更高的采用率。
content.en_text: I decided to do interviews rather than rely on distribution of the questionnaire, as I felt this was likely to lead to a higher take-up rate.

content.cn_text: 我去了四家超级超市，两家在
content.en_text: I visited four Supersave stores, two in

content.cn_text: 一个在市中心，两个在郊区，我总共采访了101名受访者。最后，我分析了结果。
content.en_text: the city centre and two in the outskirts and altogether I interviewed 101 respondents.Then finally, I analysed the results.

content.cn_text: 我发现问卷调查的结果非常有用。
content.en_text: I found the results of the questionnaires to be very informative.

content.cn_text: 我发现，在所调查的客户总数中，64%的人在某个阶段遇到过超级商店的问题。
content.en_text: I found that out of the total number of customers investigated, 64 percent had at some stage encountered a problem in a Supersave Store.

content.cn_text: 在这些人中，绝大多数人说他们没有向任何工作人员报告这个问题，他们只是自己保密。
content.en_text: Out of these people, the vast majority said that they hadn't reported the problem to any member of staff they'd just kept it to themselves.

content.cn_text: 接下来我想弄清楚的是他们为什么没有抱怨。
content.en_text: The next thing I tried to find out was why they hadn't complained.

content.cn_text: 我采访的人中大约有25%的人说原因是他们不想被打扰，还有一小部分人说他们没有足够的时间，但55%的人说原因是他们感到害怕。
content.en_text: Well, about 25 percent of the people I interviewed said the reason was that they couldn't be bothered, and a slightly smaller percentage said they didn't have enough time, but 55 percent said the reason was that they felt intimidated.

content.cn_text: 最后，我问他们，如果不用面对面交流，他们是否会更有可能抱怨，几乎所有人都说会——确切地说，是95%。
content.en_text: I finally asked if they would be more likely to complain if they didn't have to do it face-to-face, and nearly everyone I asked said that they would - 95 percent, to be exact.

content.cn_text: 然后我着手设计网站来满足这些需求。
content.en_text: I then set about designing the website to meet these needs.

content.cn_text: 一旦我完成了这个网站，我又和邓恩先生约了一次时间，想知道他对这个网站的看法。
content.en_text: Once l'd completed the website, I made another appointment with Mr Dunne, to find out what he thought of it.

content.cn_text: 邓恩先生说，他认为这些网页将使他的组织受益，因为它为客户提供了一种表达他们投诉的新方式，使收集投诉变得更容易，并确定服务和客户关怀不及应有水平的具体地方，并据此采取行动。
content.en_text: Mr Dunne said he felt that the pages would benefit his organisation by giving customers a new way of expressing their complaints, and by making it easier to collect complaints, identify specific places where service and customer care were not as good as they should be, and act upon them accordingly.

content.cn_text: Supersave已经是一个高度以客户为导向的组织，他认为我们的网站将是他们客户服务工作的一个很好的补充。
content.en_text: Supersave is already a highly customer-orientated organisation and he thought our website would be an excellent addition to their customer care effort.

content.cn_text: 这一切都很好，但网站仍然存在一个普遍的问题，那就是缺乏上网的机会。
content.en_text: This is all well and good but there still remains the general problem with websites, that there's a lack of access to on-line computers.

content.cn_text: 令人惊讶的是，在我的调查中，我发现88%的受访者可以上网，我觉得这个比例相当高。
content.en_text: Surprisingly, in my survey I found that 88 percent of those interviewed had access to the Internet, which I felt was quite high.

content.cn_text: 但对一些人来说，这种接触并不总是直接的，而是通过他们的子女、孙辈和邻居等等，而不是在他们自己家里就能轻易获得。
content.en_text: But this access wasn't always direct for some people it was through their children and grandchildren and neighbours and so on, rather than being readily available in their own homes.

content.cn_text: 这可能被证明是该网站的一个主要缺点，但现在拥有它仍然比竞争对手有优势，无论多么微弱，并且在不久的将来，预计几乎每个人都可以直接访问互联网。
content.en_text: This could prove to be a major drawback to the site, but it is still better to have it now to yet the edge over competitors, however slight, and ii the very near future it is expected that almost everyone will have direct access to the Internet.

content.cn_text: 另一件需要考虑的事情是，目前我只能根据从超市顾客群中收集的一小部分数据得出结论。
content.en_text: Another thing to consider is that at the moment I can only base our conclusions on data gathered from a tiny fraction of the supermarket's customer base.

content.cn_text: 为了更好地了解网站的运行情况，并了解我达到目标的程度，该网站至少需要运行几个月。
content.en_text: In order to get a better idea of how the site is doing and to see how well l have met my objectives, the site will need to have been up and running for at least a few months.

content.cn_text: 在这段时间之后，我将有可能看到人们是否真的在使用这个网站，以及它是否有助于改善他们的客户服务。
content.en_text: After this time, I'll be possible to see whether or not people are actually using the site, and if it's helping to make improvements to their customer service.

content.cn_text: 研究这个网站对超市员工的影响也会很有趣。随着越来越多的投诉涌入，士气可能会受到打击。
content.en_text: It would also be interesting to study the effect of the site on staff at the supermarket.Morale could be dented, as more complaints come in.

content.cn_text: 员工可能会觉得自己受到了不公平的批评，客户不需要另一种方式来抱怨。
content.en_text: Staff may feel they are being unfairly criticised and that there is no need for another way for customers to complain.

content.cn_text: 而且，网站还可以通过让员工团结起来克服建设性的批评来提高士气，他们可能会因为知道自己对客户有影响而获得更多的工作满意度。
content.en_text: But also, the site could boost morale by making staff come together to overcome theconstructive criticism, and they may gain more job satisfaction by knowing that they are making a difference to the customer.

content.cn_text: 所以，总的来说，我觉得我的网站已经达到了我的目标，但还有改进和扩展的余地。
content.en_text: So, overall, l feel my website has met my objectives, but there is scope for improvement and expansion.

content.cn_text: 有什么问题吗?
content.en_text: Are there any questions?

groups.desc:
Questions 31-34
Complete the flow chart below.
Write ONE WORD for each answer
groups.collect:
Research methodology
Discussion with supermarket department manager to decide on the store's
$15800$ for the website
Stage
 1 
Decision to investigate website use as a $15801$ way for customers to communicate problems
Design of questionnaire to identify customers' experiences and $15802$ to problems
Stage
 2 
Data collected from $15803$ with customers in four branches of the supermarket
Analysis of responses

items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是"requirements"，因为文中明确提到了作者与超市的客户服务部门负责人Mr Dunne进行了讨论，目的是确定超市的需求（requirements）。这个词恰好符合题干中要求填写的内容，即"supermarket department manager to decide on the store's [   ] for the website"。在商业语境中，"requirements"通常用来表示一个项目或产品所需满足的具体需求或条件，这与题目所描述的情况完全吻合。此外，"identify the requirements"是一个常见的商业术语，特别是在项目开发的初始阶段，这进一步证实了"requirements"是最合适的答案。

这道题的关键在于理解客户沟通问题的方式。文中提到了"in a private manner"，这正是题目中所需要填写的答案"private"。选择"private"作为答案是因为：

1. 它直接对应了题干中要求的"way for customers to communicate problems"。
2. 文中强调了这种沟通方式的私密性，这与面对面投诉的公开性形成对比。
3. "private"既符合了题干要求的单词数量（ONE WORD），又准确描述了这种沟通方式的性质。

记住，在雅思听力中，我们常常需要通过同义词或近义表达来找到答案。这里，"communicate problems"在原文中被表述为"get in touch"和"bring the problem to their attention"。通过识别这些表达，你就能更容易地定位到正确的答案句。同时，注意听辨描述沟通方式的形容词，这往往就是我们需要的答案。

这道题的关键在于理解问卷调查的两个主要方面：顾客的经历和态度。文中明确提到了"experiences of problems"和"attitudes towards making complaints"，这正好对应了题干中所要求填写的两个要素。"attitudes"是正确答案，因为它直接对应题干中的空缺，表示顾客对问题的态度或看法。此外，"attitudes"是复数形式，与题干中的"experiences"保持一致，符合平行结构。所以，尽管文中还提到了其他相关词如"experiences"，但"attitudes"才是最符合题意和语境的答案。

这道题的关键在于理解研究者实际使用的数据收集方法。题干中提到"Data collected from ... with customers in four branches of the supermarket"，这给了我们一个重要线索。在听力材料中，研究者虽然先提到了设计问卷，但随后明确表示选择了进行面试而不是分发问卷。他说："I decided to do interviews rather than rely on distribution of the questionnaire"，这清楚地表明了他的选择。接着，他还提到："I visited four Supersave stores...and altogether I interviewed 101 respondents"，这进一步确认了他在四家超市进行了面试。因此，正确答案是"interviews"，因为这是研究者实际用来收集数据的方法。记住，在这类题目中，我们需要关注的是实际执行的行动，而不是计划阶段的想法。

groups.desc:
Questions 35-40
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

items.title: Which pie chart shows the percentage of respondents who experienced a problem in the supermarket?
items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B，因为原文中明确指出64%的受访者在Supersave超市遇到过问题。题目要求选择正确显示遇到问题的受访者百分比的饼图，而64%正好对应于选项B中的饼图。这个数据直接回答了题目中询问的"经历过问题的受访者的百分比"。

items.title: Which pie chart shows the reasons why customers failed to report the problem directly to supermarket staff?
items.analysis: 文中明确给出了三个顾客没有直接向超市员工报告问题的原因及其对应的百分比：25%的人觉得麻烦，略少于25%的人没有时间，55%的人感到受到威胁。这三个百分比正好对应了选项C中饼图的分布。因此，Chart C最准确地反映了顾客未报告问题的原因分布。

items.title: How might the student's website help the supermarket, according to the manager?

options.title: A
options.content: It would support the expansion of the company.

options.title: B
options.content: It would allow the identification of problem areas.

options.title: C
options.content: It would make the company appear more professional

items.analysis: 根据原文，Dunne先生明确指出网站将帮助超市识别具体哪些地方的服务和客户关怀存在问题。这直接对应了选项B"It would allow the identification of problem areas"（它将允许识别问题区域）。网站通过收集客户投诉，使超市能够准确定位需要改进的具体领域，从而有针对性地提升服务质量。

items.title: The student says one problem is that some customers

options.title: B
options.content: do not have their own computer.

options.title: C
options.content: do not have access to a computer.

items.analysis: 文中提到虽然大多数受访者都能访问互联网，但并非所有人都能在自己家中直接访问。有些人需要通过子女、孙辈或邻居来访问互联网，这表明他们没有自己的电脑。因此，B选项"do not have their own computer"最准确地描述了这个问题。

items.title: Further observation of website use is necessary because of

options.title: A
options.content: the small size of the sample.

options.title: B
options.content: the need to evaluate the objectives.

options.title: C
options.content: the unrepresentative nature of the respondents.

items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A，因为原文中明确提到当前的结论只基于"tiny fraction"（极小部分）的顾客数据。这表明样本量太小，不足以得出全面准确的结论。因此，需要进一步观察网站的使用情况，以获得更具代表性的数据。这直接对应了选项A中提到的"small size of the sample"（样本规模小）。

items.title: One positive result of the website for the supermarket staff could be
items.analysis: 文中提到网站可能会"boost morale by making staff come together to overcome the constructive criticism"（通过让员工团结起来克服建设性批评来提高士气）。这里的"come together"（团结起来）直接对应了选项C中的"greater cooperation"（更多合作）。此外，"boost morale"（提高士气）表明这是一个积极的结果，符合题目要求的"positive result"（积极结果）。因此，C选项是正确答案。
